steps.title: Track the contrast the passage sets up
steps.content: Beaumont pushed actors to behave naturally and even hung a "Be Natural" sign. The passage then notes that silent-era actors generally relied on highly exaggerated movements and expressions because films had no sound.

steps.title: Predict what fills the blank
steps.content: Her naturalistic style stood opposite the prevailing exaggerated style of the period, so the blank should say her acting style was atypical for that era.

steps.title: Test the choices against that prediction
steps.content:
• Choice A — copied by many of Beaumont's peers.: the text never says peers imitated her; this is an overreach beyond what the passage supports. ✗
  • Choice B — familiar to actors who had worked on other directors' films.: contradicts the passage, which says actors of the era tended toward exaggeration, so naturalistic acting would be unfamiliar. ✗
  • Choice C — very unusual for the period.: matches the contrast the passage builds — her naturalism vs. the era's exaggerated norm. ✔
  • Choice D — better than film acting today.: the passage makes no comparison to modern acting; this is outside the scope. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
